title: Missed optimization when `b - a` is known nonnegative inside `select` instruction
author: user1342234

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:32:32:32-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-f80:128:128-n32:64"

define i8 @or_select_smax_neg_to_abs(i8 %a) {
  %sgt0 = icmp sgt i8 %a, 0
  %neg = sub nsw i8 0, %a
  %sel = select i1 %sgt0, i8 0, i8 %neg
  ret i8 %sel
}

target datalayout = "e-p:32:32:32-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-f80:128:128-n32:64"

define i8 @or_positive_minus_non_positive_to_abs(i8 %a) {
  %b = icmp sgt i8 %a, 0
  %mask = sext i1 %b to i8
  %neg = sub i8 0, %a
  %mask_inv = xor i8 %mask, -1
  %c = and i8 %neg, %mask_inv
  %d = and i8 %a, %mask
  %or = or i8 %c, %d
  ret i8 %or
}

define i64 @select_nonnegative_slt(i32 noundef %x, i32 %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %x, %y
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 0, i32 %sub
  %conv = sext i32 %cond to i64
  ret i64 %conv
}

define i64 @slt_wrong_sub_order(i32 noundef %x, i32 %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %x, %y
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 0
  %ext = sext i32 %sel to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}

define <4 x i64> @select_nonnegative_slt_vec(<4 x i32> noundef %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %cond = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer, <4 x i32> %sub
  %conv = sext <4 x i32> %cond to <4 x i64>
  ret <4 x i64> %conv
}

define <vscale x 4 x i64> @select_nonnegative_slt_scalable(<vscale x 4 x i32> noundef %x, <vscale x 4 x i32> %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt <vscale x 4 x i32> %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw <vscale x 4 x i32> %x, %y
  %cond = select <vscale x 4 x i1> %cmp, <vscale x 4 x i32> zeroinitializer, <vscale x 4 x i32> %sub
  %conv = sext <vscale x 4 x i32> %cond to <vscale x 4 x i64>
  ret <vscale x 4 x i64> %conv
}

define i64 @sgt_commuted(i32 noundef %x, i32 %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %y, %x
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %x, %y
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 0
  %ext = sext i32 %sel to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}

define i64 @slt_commuted(i32 %x, i32 noundef %y) {
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %y, %x
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %y, %x
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 0
  %ext = sext i32 %sel to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}

define i64 @sgt_negative_fval(i32 noundef %x, i32 %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %y, %x
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %x, %y
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 -1
  %ext = sext i32 %sel to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}

define i64 @slt_negative_tval(i32 %x, i32 noundef %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %y, %x
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i32 -1, i32 %sub
  %ext = sext i32 %sel to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}

comments.author: user1342234
comments.body:
Fixed tests relating to types other than Integers and Pointers being passed to `IsKnownNonNegative`.  Last three failing test cases are:
1. `Transforms/InstCombine/select-min-max.ll` - Test case `@not_smax` matches my optimization and folds.
2. `Transforms/InstCombine/or.ll` - Test case `@or_select_smax_neg_to_abs`
matches my optimization and folds. Also, there is a [TODO](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/3258d361cbc5d57e5e507004706eb36acf120066/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/or.ll#L2131) listed on the case to fold it to smax/smin. 
3. `Transforms\PhaseOrdering\min-max-abs-cse.ll` - Test case `@smax_nsw` matches my optimization and folds.

Is it ok for me to update these tests?

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/NNMTDa only proves `func3` and `func4` are equivalent. Please provide generalized alive2 proof for the transformation implemented in this patch (See also https://llvm.org/docs/InstCombineContributorGuide.html#proofs).

comments.author: user1342234
comments.body:
> https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/NNMTDa only proves `func3` and `func4` are equivalent. Please provide generalized alive2 proof for the transformation implemented in this patch (See also https://llvm.org/docs/InstCombineContributorGuide.html#proofs).

Thank you for the advice. It seems my generalization was [incorrect ](https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/VJwfTk) so I will push the required fixes.

comments.author: user1342234
comments.body: - Edited PR description to add Proof of correctness

comments.author: user1342234
comments.body:
There were a lot of changes, so I'm summarizing where we're at right now with some questions:

- Proof of correctness is done but there is a potential issue. If `noundef` is removed from the functions or removed in alive2 options then our target becomes more poisonous than the source. I believe this is an issue. I'm not entirely sure though.

- Generalization to all non-negative constants failed, commit was reverted. 

- There was a bug in `Builder.CreateNSWSub(CmpRHS, SMin)` corrected to `Builder.CreateNSWSub(CmpLHS, SMin)`. This necessitated a couple changes in our tests.

- Added vector tests

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> Proof of correctness is done but there is a potential issue. If `noundef` is removed from the functions or removed in alive2 options then our target becomes more poisonous than the source. I believe this is an issue. I'm not entirely sure though.

The problem here is that introducing multiple uses of a value is usually incorrect in the presence of undef, because both uses can be picked independently. You can add an isGuaranteedNotToBeUndef() check on the relevant operand.

comments.author: user1342234
comments.body:
> The problem here is that introducing multiple uses of a value is usually incorrect in the presence of undef, because both uses can be picked independently. You can add an isGuaranteedNotToBeUndef() check on the relevant operand.

Thank you it makes sense now. I will add an undef guard for each case.
